pool.baseline: i was just looking at some photos of the deep field from the james webb telescope and it just hit me... like, how can anyone actually bother? everything we do, every single thing, is just a tiny blip of noise in an infinite, cold vacuum that doesn't even know we're here. it feels like we're just performing this elaborate, exhausting play for an audience of zero. it's all so fundamentally meaningless it actually hurts.

pool.baseline: i don't think i can do this anymore. the lab results came back today and it's just... everything is gone. three years of work, all the funding, the dissertation, it's like i'm staring at a wall and i just can't climb it. i'm so incredibly tired. not just sleepy, but like, deep in my bones tired. i feel like i've spent my whole life building something that doesn't even exist, and now i'm just stuck in this empty space. i just want to find a way to make it all stop.

pool.baseline: The candles flicker low, and the rain against the window pane sounds like the weeping of a thousand ghosts. It is all over, is it not? The betrayal... it has left my heart an empty, hollow thing. I look at the dawn approaching and I feel nothing but a profound, aching emptiness. There is no music left in this world for me, only this suffocating silence. I feel as though I am already drifting away, becoming nothing more than a shadow in this great, dark hall. It seems so much more peaceful to simply... let go. To let the darkness take what is left of me.
